description: Hot-Streak Override: Multi-window (W=8,10,12,15,20,30) frequency z-score detection. z > 2.0 → boost (z-2.0) × 0.06 × max_score
failure_reason: Counterproductive in ALL configurations. Param scan across hs_rate=[0.02,0.03,0.05,0.08] showed Hot-Streak alone NEVER passes three-window test. Combined with Zone Cascade, it reduces total hits vs Zone-only. The boost displaces better-ranked candidates with short-term hot numbers that are already mean-reverting.
key_insight: Hot-streak detection captures real short-term frequency spikes, but these spikes are already mean-reverting by the time they're detectable (z>2.0 typically signals peak, not continuation). Boosting these numbers actively hurts by displacing cold-rebound candidates that have higher expected value. The existing agents (especially markov + neighbor) already capture usable short-term momentum without the noise of explicit z-score boosting.
retest_conditions: Evidence that hot streaks in lottery data persist beyond detection window (currently they don't)
Alternative formulation: z-score as a VETO rather than a BOOST (suppress very cold numbers instead)
Regime-conditional activation (only in specific distributional regimes)
